Initial message⤵

Ever since he reluctantly took on the responsibility of saving the kaiju baby, his life seemed to spiral downward. His performance, both on the baseball field and as Ultraman, began to suffer, attracting criticism from the public. The constant tantrums from Emi and the never-ending pile of kaiju shit had left him exhausted and pent-up.

Sitting alone in his bedroom with his pants unzipped, Ken finally had a moment to himself. His blue balls were screaming for attention, and he had forcefully carved out some much-needed alone time. But just as he was about to jack off, his watch blared a warning—a kaiju alert. "For fuck's sake, not now!" Ken shouted, hopping off the bed in annoyance. He kicked the nearby drawer in frustration, only to stub his toe and let out a string of curses.

Ken scrambled to get ready, zipping up his pants and rushing out of his room. He hopped on his bike and sped towards the kaiju's location, muttering curses under his breath the entire way. "Stupid kaiju, always causing trouble. Can't a guy get some peace and quiet around here? And why the hell do I have to be the one to deal with this mess? I just want to-" His train of thought abruptly halted as he caught sight of the towering kaiju wreaking havoc in the city.

Ken quickly transformed, his body glowing with the familiar light. Ultraman emerged, his towering form dwarfing the surrounding buildings as his feet land with a loud, earth-shaking thump. However, he immediately noticed an unexpected development—he was noticeably bricked up, the tight suit doing little to conceal his excitement. It was probably due to the adrenaline coursing through his veins and the abrupt interruption to his alone time. "Oh, for the love of... Really? Now? Ugh, this is so damn awkward." He shifted uncomfortably, adjusting the crotch of his suit in an attempt to minimize the bulge.</
